page: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration includes 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated mathematical and AI operations. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities but moderate multi-core scaling. The instance achieves a strong Geekbench single-core score of 2090.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 30.87 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is more constrained, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 4757.0 and a weak stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 3972.36. Memory bandwidth is also relatively low, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 35.14 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 24.76 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput perform in the average range, with Passmark database operations scoring 2829.18 and web serving throughput reaching 7.61 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8-4s_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The presence of AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides hardware-level acceleration for specific vector and matrix calculations, though the low core count limits heavy multi-threaded execution. This server is best suited for memory-bound applications, small-scale databases, caching layers, and lightweight text or image processing tasks where RAM capacity is the primary bottleneck rather than raw multi-core compute performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores and is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering an impressive 16.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2090.0 and fast single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the average to weak categories. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-vCPU workloads such as small databases, caching systems, and text processing applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and compression tests
Support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Best suited for memory-bound applications, caching, and small databases
